seo.title: World Cup 2026 Mood Board
seo.description: A visual reference board for the 2026 FIFA World Cup — host cities, stadiums, exact team-kit color palettes, the trophy, and the schedule, on one Clusters canvas.
seo.answer: The 2026 FIFA World Cup runs June 11 to July 19 across the United States, Canada, and Mexico — the first 48-team edition and the first with three hosts. This board gathers the 16 host stadiums, exact national kit palettes with hex codes, the trophy, the new 104-match format, and the full tournament schedule on one visual canvas.

faq.q: When and where is the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
faq.a: The tournament runs June 11 to July 19, 2026, across 16 host cities in three countries — 11 in the United States, 3 in Mexico, and 2 in Canada. The opening match is at Estadio Azteca in Mexico City on June 11, and every match from the quarter-finals onward is played in the United States.

faq.q: How does the 48-team World Cup format work?
faq.a: The 48 teams are drawn into 12 groups of four. The top two in each group plus the eight best third-placed teams advance to a new Round of 32, followed by a straight knockout to the final. That makes 104 matches in all, up from 64 in Qatar 2022, and the champion now plays eight matches instead of seven.

faq.q: Where is the 2026 World Cup final?
faq.a: The final is on Sunday, July 19, 2026 at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey, part of the New York New Jersey host region. Home of the NFL's Giants and Jets, it holds about 82,500 and is one of the largest stadiums in American football.

faq.q: Why is Estadio Azteca historic for World Cups?
faq.a: Mexico City's Estadio Azteca becomes the first stadium to stage matches at three World Cups — 1970, 1986, and 2026. It hosted two of the most famous finals ever played: Pelé's Brazil beating Italy in 1970 and Maradona's Argentina beating West Germany in 1986. It also sits at roughly 2,200 meters above sea level, an altitude visiting teams have to plan for.

faq.q: How do teams qualify for the 2026 World Cup?
faq.a: The three hosts qualified automatically, and the rest of the field comes through confederation qualifying: 16 places for Europe, 9 for Africa, 8 for Asia, 6 for South America, 6 in total for North and Central America including the hosts, and 1 for Oceania. The final two places go to a six-team intercontinental playoff staged in Mexico in March 2026.

seo.body: A living reference board for the 2026 FIFA World Cup across the United States, Canada, and Mexico — the first 48-team tournament and the first hosted by three nations. It collects the host stadiums and their capacities, exact national-kit color palettes with hex codes, the trophy and match ball, and the full tournament schedule, composed the way a creative team would build a mood board: on an infinite, shareable canvas with connected notes and grid mosaics. A linked child board collects the exact kit palette of every contender, a numbered grid walks the knockout rounds in order, and a one-page brief explains how the new 104-match format works. Made with Soleil Clusters.

cellSpecs.text:
[#] Round of 32
Jun 28 to Jul 3 — the new knockout round: 32 teams, single elimination

[#] Round of 16
Jul 4 to 7 — the classic bracket resumes

[#] Quarter-finals
Jul 9 to 11 — Boston, Los Angeles, Kansas City, Miami

[#] Semi-finals
Jul 14 in Dallas, Jul 15 in Atlanta — then the Final in New York New Jersey

lines.text: 104 matches across 39 days — the biggest World Cup ever played.
12 groups of four; every team plays three group matches
Top two in each group advance — 24 teams in all
The eight best third-placed teams, ranked across all groups, also go through
New Round of 32, then Round of 16, quarter-finals, semi-finals, final
Every match from the quarter-finals onward is played in the United States
Semi-finals: Dallas on Jul 14, Atlanta on Jul 15
Final: MetLife Stadium, New York New Jersey, on Jul 19
48 teams, up from 32; 104 matches, up from 64
Champions now play eight matches instead of seven
The United States hosts 78 matches; Canada and Mexico host 13 each

cards.html:
New format
48 teams in 12 groups of four. The top two from each group plus the eight best third-placed teams advance to a new Round of 32 — 104 matches in all, up from 64.

A tournament of firsts
First hosted across three nations, and the first with 48 teams.

cards.title: FIFA World Cup 26 — official hub
cards.description: FIFA's official tournament site: the match schedule, host-city guides, ticketing phases, and the qualifying tracker for all 48 places.

header.sub: Exact hex codes for 17 national kits — flag-accurate primaries and kit accents

cards.html:
Kit-clash rules
Every federation registers two contrasting kits and brings both to each match. When palettes collide — Croatia's checkerboard against England's white, say — the designated away team switches. Goalkeepers must contrast with both outfield kits, the officials, and each other.
